race.name: L'Étape Indonesia
race.tagline: Two editions in Lombok, then quietly discontinued.

vitals.location: Pertamina Mandalika International Circuit, Central Lombok, West Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia
vitals.date_specific: Last confirmed edition: May 28, 2023. A 2024 relocation to Solo, Java was announced but no completed edition is verifiable; Indonesia is absent from L'Étape's official 2026 series calendar.

climate.description: Late-May Lombok: tropical heat and humidity, with the route running from coastal circuit terrain into rolling inland hills.
challenges: Heat and humidity: tropical conditions typical of late May in Lombok

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 47. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 44. NOTE (wave 10): climbing=2 correctly matches the confirmed 1,301m of gain on the 141km long route (rubric band 500-1,500m). FLAG (wave 10, not applied): the event is now confirmed discontinued (last held 2023), which may warrant a full rescore or removal alongside gfny-paracas and tallinn-gran-fondo; road_surface=1 and organization=3 also sit awkwardly against documented 2022 road-preparation and security shortfalls. Numbers left unchanged pending human review.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this event is discontinued at this location; the profile describes a race that no longer runs in Lombok. L'Étape Indonesia started and finished at the Pertamina Mandalika International Circuit (the MotoGP venue) in Central Lombok, offering two routes: The Ride (79 km / 494 m) and The Race (141 km / 1,301 m). Official race materials describe rolling southern-Lombok roads with short, steep climbs, remote villages, rice paddies, beaches, and coastal views, with the long route's hills overlooking Lombok's southern coastline. It ran only twice — 2022 and 2023 — before disappearing from the calendar; a 2024 relocation to Solo, Java was announced but no completed edition was verifiable, and Indonesia is absent from L'Étape's official 2026 series calendar.
course_description.signature_challenge: The short, steep climbs overlooking Lombok's southern coastline on The Race's 141 km route — punchy rather than long, in tropical heat.
suffering_zones: Southern Lombok's short, steep coastal-hill sectors on the 141 km route.
Sustained tropical heat and humidity across the full distance.

logistics.airport: Lombok International Airport (LOP), the nearest commercial airport to Mandalika.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels near the Mandalika circuit area; the region is commonly combined with a Bali trip, though the event itself is discontinued.

history.origin_story: Announced in 2020 as a partnership between A.S.O. (Tour de France organizer) and local promoter Rocca, but pandemic delays pushed the first completed edition to 2022. A second edition followed in 2023 under A.S.O. with local promoter Veloism Danendra Aksata. The 2022 debut was troubled by unresolved road-preparation and security issues shortly before race day, and that edition recorded three crashes, including one rider hospitalized with a head laceration.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Real Two-Edition L'Étape Stop, Now Gone From Lombok
biased_opinion.summary: L'Étape Indonesia was a genuine entry in the official L'Étape by Tour de France series, run at Lombok's Mandalika circuit in 2022 and 2023 with two honest distance options and real A.S.O. backing. It wasn't trouble-free — the 2022 debut had documented last-minute road-preparation and security gaps, and recorded crashes including one rider hospitalized. It also wasn't durable: after two editions, the race disappeared from Lombok, a reported 2024 move to Solo never produced a verifiable completed event, and Indonesia is absent from L'Étape's current global calendar.
strengths: Official L'Étape by Tour de France series backing (A.S.O.-run), with two documented editions
Two honest distance options (79 km / 141 km) with genuine scenic variety — rice paddies, remote villages, coastal hills
A distinctive, non-European venue for the series: the Mandalika MotoGP circuit
weaknesses: Discontinued after only two editions (2022-2023) — not currently a bookable event in Lombok
The 2022 debut had documented pre-race road-condition and security shortfalls, plus multiple rider crashes
A reported 2024 relocation to Solo, Java never produced a verifiable completed edition
The former letapeindonesia.com domain is no longer a reliable source — it currently resolves to unrelated content
biased_opinion.bottom_line: This entry exists as a historical record, not a current recommendation — L'Étape Indonesia hasn't run in Lombok since 2023. If you want an active L'Étape series stop in the region, check L'Étape's official calendar (letapebytourdefrance.com) for what's currently running in Asia.

final_verdict.one_liner: A real but short-lived L'Étape stop at Lombok's Mandalika circuit — two editions, then gone.
final_verdict.should_you_race: You can't — no edition has run in Lombok since 2023, and an announced Solo, Java relocation never produced a verified completed event. Check L'Étape's official calendar for current Asia-Pacific stops instead.
final_verdict.alternatives: For an active L'Étape series stop in Asia or nearby, check letapebytourdefrance.com's current calendar directly rather than relying on this listing.

videos.title: 🇮🇩 L'Etape Indonesia 2023 | HIGHLIGHTS 🤩
videos.channel: L'Etape Series by Tour de France
videos.description:
For the second time, L’Etape took us to the pearl of Indonesia: Lombok. Empty beaches, lost small steep mountains, remote villages and rice paddies: an epic ride into the untouched wild Indonesia. 🚴

🚩 Mandalika - 🏁 Mandalika
📅 28th May 2023
📏 2 routes: 79km - 141km
🗻 494m – 1301m

👉 https://letapeindonesia.com
_____🚲__
#letape #letapeIndonesia


---------

L'Etape by Tour de France is a series of amateur cycling events designed to experience the Tour de France Legend. It is the only event where you can race and feel like a Tour de France champion, and where you can enjoy the Tour de France unique atmosphere and passion.

“The Tour de France is the world's greatest cycling race. Each year it is passionately followed by fans around the world so it has naturally given rise to L'Etape by Tour de France series”, says Christian Prudhomme, Tour de France director. 

The series has now expanded to more than 20 international locations including:
🇫🇷 France
🇧🇷 Brasil
🇲🇽 México
🇨🇴 Colombia
🇨🇳 China
🇦🇺 Australia
🇹🇭 Thailand
🇨🇭 Switzerland
🇮🇩 Indonesia
🇪🇸 Spain
🇲🇾 Malaysia
🇸🇰 Slovakia
🇨🇿 Czech Republic
🇬🇧 UK
🇨🇦 Canada
🇺🇸 USA
🇳🇱 Netherlands
🇨🇾 Cyprus
🇸🇮 Slovenia
🇪🇨 Ecuador

Among these Tour de France official cycling events is of course L'Étape du Tour. With a peloton of 15 000 riders and 50 nationalities, it is Europe's biggest cycling event. Riding on the same routes and under the same conditions as Tour de France champions: this is the promise that L'Étape du Tour has kept since 1993.

Check out https://www.letapebytourdefrance.com/ for more information.

Join us on:
-Facebook 👉 https://www.facebook.com/letapebytourdefrance
-Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/letapebytourdefrance/
-Strava 👉 https://www.strava.com/clubs/letapebyletourdefrance
